notes: mazdausa.com/vehicles/<model> consumer site. Hybrid/PHEV variants share the parent model page (cx-50-hybrid, cx-70-phev). MX-5 Miata RF is the targa-roof variant. Phase B 2026-05-14: all 12 URLs were 404 with the year-prefixed pattern (/vehicles/2026-<model>); site now uses unprefixed /vehicles/<model> — all 12 updated and verified OK. Session 6 (2026-05-14): added asset-naming variants — many image filenames/alt-text use spelled-out forms (`mazda-3-sedan`, `mazda-3-hatchback`), shorthand SKU codes (`m3hb`, `m3-sedan`, `mx-5-st`, `mx-5-rf`), and no-dash filename forms (`cx30`, `cx50`, `cx70`, `cx90`) that the bare model slug did not cover. Blacklist tightened to drop the shopping-icons, interior-swatches and global-nav cross-page chrome that was generating 30+ unmatched candidates per page. Collision note: kept the parent-slug variants (`cx-5`, `cx-50`, `cx-70`, `cx-90`, `mx-5-miata`) WITHOUT looser overlaps that would bleed into their hybrid/phev/rf siblings; each sibling has its own explicit variants instead. Session 7 Phase A3 (2026-05-14): added angle_url_patterns. Mazda's CDN names its 3/4 trim renders inside `/04_btv/001_trims/34-jellies/` (and its `06_btv/cx-90-inline/001_trims/34-jellies/` legacy variant) — Phase 3 noted this folder was a strong front_three_quarter cue but `34` is a prefix not a `front`-anchored suffix, so the shared ANGLE_PATTERNS could not see it. Image-content verification (sampled 10+ jellies): every `siteassets/.../001_trims/34-jellies/` render IS a 3/4 front studio shot. CX-5 is the exception: its 2026 redesign published the jellies on the alternate `/content/dam/musa/vehicle-assets/.../001-trims/34-jellies/` path with all-dash separators, and ALL 5 of those renders are SIDE PROFILE views (verified). Underscore-vs-dash inside `0\d_btv/001_trims` vs `04-btv/001-trims` is the precise discriminator between the two angle classes and is the basis for the regexes here. No reliable rear_three_quarter token exists: hero shots are inconsistent (cx-30/cx-50/m3-sedan hero = 3/4 front; cx-70/cx-90/mx-5-rf hero = 3/4 rear or rear), and the inline-desktop-carousel sequence indexes do not correlate with angle. interior_dashboard already works via the existing English vocabulary (`interior` / `cabin` regexes hit `/interior-360s/` URL segments and 'Interior Cabin' alt text); no new patterns added there.
